---
title: Inputs & Outputs Demo
---

# Inputs & Outputs

This runbook demonstrates the new template syntax for referencing inputs and outputs in block props.

## The New Syntax

| Namespace | Syntax | Example |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| Inputs | `{{ .inputs.variableName }}` | `{{ .inputs.repo_url }}` |
| Outputs | `{{ .outputs.block_id.outputName }}` | `{{ .outputs.create_account.account_id }}` |

Both namespaces are explicit — no ambiguity about where a value comes from.

---

## Step 1: Configure Clone Parameters

Define the repository details as user inputs. The GitClone block below will read these values and pre-fill its form fields.

<Inputs id="clone-config">
```yaml
variables:
  - name: repo_org
    type: string
    description: The GitHub organization of the repository to clone
    default: "gruntwork-io"
    validations:
      - required
  - name: repo_name
    type: string
    description: The name of the GitHub repository to clone
    default: "runbooks-test"
    validations:
      - required
  - name: branch
    type: string
    description: Branch or tag to check out
    default: main
```
</Inputs>

## Step 2: Clone from Inputs

Use `inputsId` to connect a block to an Inputs block. Then reference variables with `{{ .inputs.variableName }}` — the values are resolved reactively as the user types.

### How it works

The `inputsId` prop tells GitClone where to read its values from:

```
inputsId="clone-config"    ← matches the id="clone-config" Inputs above
```

Each `{{ .inputs.* }}` expression maps to a variable defined in that Inputs block:

```
prefilledUrl="https://github.com/{{ .inputs.repo_org }}/{{ .inputs.repo_name }}"
                                   ↑                      ↑
                                   repo_org = "gruntwork-io"  repo_name = "runbooks-test"

prefilledRef="{{ .inputs.branch }}"
                  ↑
                  branch = "main"
```

Template expressions work in any string prop — not just form fields. Here we also use them in `title` and `description`:

```
title="Clone {{ .inputs.repo_org }}/{{ .inputs.repo_name }}"
  → "Clone gruntwork-io/runbooks-test"
```

### Try it

<GitClone
  id="clone-from-inputs"
  inputsId="clone-config"
  prefilledUrl="https://github.com/{{ .inputs.repo_org }}/{{ .inputs.repo_name }}"
  prefilledRef="{{ .inputs.branch }}"
  title="Clone {{ .inputs.repo_org }}/{{ .inputs.repo_name }}"
  description="This will clone the branch {{ .inputs.branch }} from the repository {{ .inputs.repo_org }}/{{ .inputs.repo_name }}"
/>

Change the input values in Step 1 — the GitClone form fields, title, and description all update in real time.

---

## Step 3: Multiple Inputs Sources

A block can pull values from more than one Inputs block by passing an **array** of IDs to `inputsId`. This is useful when you have shared defaults that individual steps can override.

### How it works

When `inputsId` is an array, values merge left-to-right. If two Inputs blocks define the same variable, the **later** one wins:

```
inputsId={["defaults", "overrides"]}
```

```
defaults:    { repo_url: "https://...", branch: "main" }
overrides:   { branch: "feature/new-syntax" }
                                ↓
merged:      { repo_url: "https://...", branch: "feature/new-syntax" }
```

### Try it

Below are two Inputs blocks. The "Defaults" block sets `repo_url` and `branch`. The "Overrides" block redefines `branch` only. The GitClone block merges both — so `repo_url` comes from Defaults, but `branch` comes from Overrides.

<Inputs id="defaults">
```yaml
variables:
  - name: repo_url
    type: string
    description: Default repository URL
    default: "https://github.com/gruntwork-io/runbooks"
  - name: branch
    type: string
    description: Default branch
    default: main
```
</Inputs>

<Inputs id="overrides">
```yaml
variables:
  - name: branch
    type: string
    description: Override branch (takes precedence over defaults)
    default: feature/new-syntax
```
</Inputs>

<GitClone
  id="clone-merged"
  inputsId={["defaults", "overrides"]}
  prefilledUrl="{{ .inputs.repo_url }}"
  prefilledRef="{{ .inputs.branch }}"
  title="Clone with Merged Inputs"
  description="repo_url from 'defaults', branch from 'overrides'"
/>

Notice the Git URL field shows the repo URL from **Defaults**, while the Ref field shows **feature/new-syntax** from **Overrides**. Try changing the branch in either Inputs block to see which one takes effect.
